steps.title: Find the definition clue
steps.content: The notes give a direct definition: “Lazarus species” is a term for living species once believed to be extinct.

steps.title: Apply the definition to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A states that the term “Lazarus species” describes a living species of organism, such as the black-footed ferret, that was once believed to be extinct, which matches the definition and supplies the requested example.

The other three choices either do not define the term, lack the example, or only give an example without the definition.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
